name: Side-Lying External Rotation
description: Lie on your side with the working arm on top. Elbow bent at 90 degrees, tucked against your side. Starting with your forearm pointing toward the floor, rotate your forearm up toward the ceiling while keeping your elbow pinned to your side. Control back down slowly. No weight needed — gravity provides the resistance. Targets the infraspinatus and teres minor (external rotators of the rotator cuff).
